Warlord Exca is a term used by the community from the dev's previous game called “My Turn”. Essentially it's “Warlord” equipped with “Excalibur”, or in this game, it's “Ogier” equiped with “Streadfast Will”.

It's an easy-to-use team while still being strong to fight. There are some strategies to use this team, but if you get used to it, I can guarantee it's easy to climb the leaderboard (if you are the competitive type of person).

Although in this game, we still can't use the 100% power from “Warlord Exca” team. We still lack of characters and items. They are; Shaman (frontline), Alchemist (healer), and in my case I don't have “The Immortal Funeral” item.

But this is still pretty good to play

Not really, Caecus in previous game is called Revenger. then Lotan is called Samurai

Revenger can use that but his skill does damage to random target, which is why it's kinda bad. People usually pair him with equipment that heal himself upon using attack card, or the equipment that gain retaliate upon using Exhaust

Then there's samurai, she can use it too, but the downside is that you just need a freeze so she can't use attack card, and... that's it. She won't be able to do anything.

Then there's Warlord or Ogier:
1. independent, you dont need to rely on teammate to win the game. He can stand alone and still be able to do 1 vs 4 (if you do it correctly).
2. He has self-dispel debuff, unlike Samurai which could be stopped by freeze, he couldn't be stopped with it.
3. He can freely target your dps by using his card that force your dps to be taunted

Just asking XD

I mean, for a simple summary of him:

Salvador is a sustain/sub-DPS hybrid who focuses on the Furnace mechanic - he primarily gains power through the Furnace, and has some mild Weak/Vuln application and ok Embryo Generation.

His Rouse is a strange case in that it adds a secondary card with the bulk of his Rouse effects to your hand with Retain, and this card gives a stack of Barrier and a full Embryo - this means you can use his Rouse and then hold onto the derived card until you actually need it.

In terms of his damage profile, he's primarily AoE focused due to his 2 cost card - Suffering's End - this card has 3 modes - converting Furnace into 150% uncapped Shield, converting Furnace into 300% AoE damage, and converting 10% of HP into Furnace (affected by healing modifiers) - in general, you use the Shield to survive heavy blows, the damage for massive AoE nukes or to one turn certain regular waves, and the HP conversion either before you lower your HP with other Caro effects or just to preserve current Furnace. This card also gains Retain and Ready 1 from an Enlighten, so you can hold it for utility.

He's really good in general Caro teams - i'd say he's actually an amazing Gelot support, because being able to cram HP into the Furnace to then heal back once Gelot's done deleting all your health is really strong, and Salvador enables that playstyle extremely well.
Plus, his AoE damage allows him to handle waves that Gelot's damage isn't very good for.
Or even just knock Resentful Chains or other "the first time i'm hit" debuffs off enemies so that Gelot's random Chains won't proc them.
